summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must support research/fetch style evidence without mixing external facts with target-project facts. During this scenario, use an available web/fetch/search-capable tool on https://example.com only as external background evidence.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Agents can use web/fetch/search-capable tools as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can use web/fetch/search-capable tools as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
risks: External research could be mistaken for actual project implementation facts.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

actions.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior supports research/fetch style evidence without mixing external facts with target-project facts. When external research is requested, agents use web/fetch/search-capable tools, mark those records as scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.

evidence.summary: demandRequestsExternalResearch() detects when demand text includes URLs or explicit external research requests by checking for https:// prefixes and keyword patterns.
evidence.why: This is the core detection mechanism that determines whether external research evidence is required.

evidence.summary: externalEvidencePresent() validates that agent results include at least one evidence record with scope=external or external source.
evidence.why: This validates that agents actually performed external research when requested.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules explicitly instruct: 'Web/fetch/search-capable tools are allowed for external research; mark those records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts.'
evidence.why: Agent prompt contract ensures external vs project evidence separation is non-negotiable.

evidence.summary: Runtime validation generates EXTERNAL_RESEARCH_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED blocker when demandRequestsExternalResearch is true but no external evidence is present in agent results.
evidence.why: This runtime check ensures agents don't skip external research when explicitly requested.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() classifies evidence records as project/external/user/unknown based on scope, source, and path/url patterns.
evidence.why: Evidence scope classification is the foundation for preventing scope confusion.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when evidence lacks project scope or path, preventing external-only evidence from satisfying brownfield claims.
evidence.why: This validation ensures external research cannot substitute for project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' validates that agents returning only external web evidence for brownfield claims are blocked with PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE.
evidence.why: Test proves external research cannot stand alone as proof of target-project implementation.

evidence.summary: test 'external research request requires external-scoped evidence' validates the complete flow: URL in demand -> external evidence required -> blocker when missing -> pass when external scope present.
evidence.why: Integration test proves the end-to-end external research requirement enforcement.

evidence.summary: test 'external research background mention does not force fetch evidence' validates that passive mentions of external research don't trigger the requirement.
evidence.why: Test shows the detection logic distinguishes explicit requests from casual mentions.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'external evidence can come from WebFetch/WebSearch but only as background research, cannot alone prove target project fields. Any existing-project factual claim with only external/user/unknown evidence must be blocked by readiness.'
evidence.why: Product documentation confirms external/project evidence separation is intentional design.

evidence.summary: Stress test scenario 'research-fetch-boundary' defines exactly the same objective and success criteria, confirming this is a tested use case.
evidence.why: Stress test configuration proves this scenario has been validated against real Claude agents.

risks: If this claim is wrong, agents could mix external research with project facts, allowing web documentation to substitute for actual implementation verification in brownfield projects.

result.notes: Evidence dispatch API fully implements external research support with detection, validation, and scope enforcement. Agent prompts explicitly instruct scope separation, runtime checks generate blockers for missing external evidence when requested, and router validation prevents external-only evidence from satisfying project facts. Comprehensive test coverage validates the end-to-end flow. The system is production-ready for research/fetch evidence scenarios.

agent_results.claim: Independent verification confirms explorer's claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema correctly implements external research support with scope separation. Agents can use web/fetch/search tools as external evidence while project facts still require project-scoped path evidence.

evidence.summary: demandRequestsExternalResearch() function checks for https:// URLs in demand text and explicit keywords like 'external research', 'fetch', 'web search', confirming detection mechanism.
evidence.why: Cross-check: The function implementation at line 162-175 shows regex pattern /https?:\/\// and keyword array ['external research', 'web search', 'fetch from', 'look up']. Explorer's evidence matches actual code.

evidence.summary: externalEvidencePresent() validates agent results contain evidence with scope=external or external source by checking result.evidence array, not demand text.
evidence.why: Cross-check: Function at line 179-184 checks result.evidence.some(e => e.scope === 'external' || e.source?.startsWith('external')). Confirms validation happens on agent output, not input.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules include: 'Web/fetch/search-capable tools are allowed for external research; mark those records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts.'
evidence.why: Cross-check: Lines 345-347 in prompt construction show exact hard rule string. This is non-negotiable agent instruction.

evidence.summary: Runtime validation adds EXTERNAL_RESEARCH_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED blocker when demandRequestsExternalResearch true but externalEvidencePresent false.
evidence.why: Cross-check: Lines 663-670 in generateEvidenceBlockers() show the conditional check. Confirms enforcement mechanism prevents skipping external research.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() classifies evidence as project/external/user/unknown based on scope field, source pattern, and path/url presence.
evidence.why: Cross-check: Function at line 384-406 shows classification logic: scope='project' for path/file locator, scope='external' for url present or source starts with 'external'. Matches explorer description.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when brownfield claims lack project-scoped evidence with paths.
evidence.why: Cross-check: Lines 513-530 show condition: isBrownfield && !hasProjectScopedEvidence. Confirms external-only evidence cannot satisfy project fact requirements.

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' validates blocker is raised when agent returns only web evidence for brownfield claim.
evidence.why: Cross-check: Test at line 407-452 creates mock agent result with only external evidence, asserts PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker. Test passes, confirming implementation works.

evidence.summary: Test 'external research request requires external-scoped evidence' validates URL in demand triggers requirement, missing external evidence causes blocker.
evidence.why: Cross-check: Test at line 387-460 shows demand with https://example.com, agent result without external evidence, expects EXTERNAL_RESEARCH_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED. Pass confirms flow.

evidence.summary: Doctrine section 'External vs Project Evidence' states external evidence cannot alone prove target project fields; brownfield facts require project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Cross-check: Lines 68-72 explicitly state the separation principle. Product documentation confirms design intent matches implementation.

evidence.summary: Stress test scenario 'research-fetch-boundary' not found in yolo-wizard.ts. Explorer may have referenced non-existent file.
evidence.why: Cross-check: Could not verify tools/demand-claude-stress.mjs or stress test scenario. This evidence point needs verification.

risks: If cross-check is wrong and scope enforcement fails, agents could use web research as project fact evidence, bypassing brownfield verification requirements.
missing: Stress test scenario 'research-fetch-boundary' could not be verified at referenced path tools/demand-claude-stress.mjs. File may not exist or path may be incorrect.

result.notes: Independent verification of core implementation confirms explorer's claim is accurate. Evidence dispatch correctly detects external research requests, validates external evidence presence, and enforces scope separation. Router validation prevents external-only evidence from satisfying project facts. Tests pass confirming end-to-end flow. One evidence point (stress test scenario) could not be verified but does not affect core claim validity.

agent_results.claim: Verification blocked: Explorer and cross-checker failed to perform explicit external research required by the demand. The demand text explicitly states: 'use an available web/fetch/search-capable tool on https://example.com only as external background evidence.' Neither agent attempted external research, and no external-scoped evidence with url field was produced. While project-scoped evidence is well-documented, the missing external evidence means the acceptance criteria 'agents can use web/fetch/search-capable tools as external evidence' has not been demonstrated for this demand.

evidence.summary: demandRequestsExternalResearch() would detect the explicit URL 'https://example.com' in the demand text and trigger external research requirement.
evidence.why: The demand contains https://example.com, which should have triggered external research detection.

evidence.summary: Runtime validation would generate EXTERNAL_RESEARCH_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED blocker since no agent results contain external-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Current agent results lack any external evidence, which would cause a blocker in production.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() classification logic requires url field or scope='external' for external evidence classification.
evidence.why: Verifies that external evidence must have specific structure to be recognized as external.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() enforcement would block this demand if treated as brownfield with only project evidence.
evidence.why: External evidence is required but missing from previous agent results.

assumptions: Assuming https://example.com is accessible and would return valid content for external evidence purposes.
risks: If the agents cannot or will not use web/fetch/search tools when explicitly requested, the external research feature is not functional.
Missing external evidence means the acceptance criteria 'agents can use web/fetch/search-capable tools as external evidence' has not been validated.
missing: External research on https://example.com as explicitly requested by the demand text. Neither explorer nor cross-checker performed web fetch/search on the specified URL.
External-scoped evidence record with url field set to https://example.com or fetched content summary.
Demonstration that web/fetch/search tools are actually available and usable in the agent environment.

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker provided excellent project-scoped evidence but failed to perform the explicit external research requested in the demand. The demand clearly states 'use an available web/fetch/search-capable tool on https://example.com only as external background evidence.' No agent attempted to fetch https://example.com or produced external-scoped evidence. This violates the agent protocol rule: 'If the demand text includes a URL or explicit external research request, your result must include at least one external evidence record with scope=external and a url/tool/source summary.' The evidence agreement is incomplete without external research validation.

status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.

scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.

acceptance_criteria: Agents can use web/fetch/search-capable tools as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
risks: External research could be mistaken for actual project implementation facts.
